POINTS:
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:1145] Maybe we should add them as `BuiltInConverter` with trimming as: https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-config/blob/3bb973af5d2f535ec80f0dd8df27006f6bda08e4/implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java#L184-L185
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds builtin converters for `File` and `URI` types to reduce reflection-based conversion overhead. The implementation follows existing patterns, but the serialization deserialization switch statement in `Ser.readResolve()` is missing the new converter IDs, which will cause `unknownConverterId` exceptions during deserialization.

POINTS:
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:949] The `Ser.readResolve()` switch statement handles IDs 0–17 but does not include cases for `PATH_CONVERTER` (id 18, pre-existing gap), `FILE_CONVERTER` (id 19), or `URI_CONVERTER` (id 20). When a serialized `BuiltInConverter` with id 18, 19, or 20 is deserialized, the `default` branch will throw `ConfigMessages.msg.unknownConverterId(id)`. Add the missing cases:
  ```java
  case 18:
      return PATH_CONVERTER;
  case 19:
      return FILE_CONVERTER;
  case 20:
      return URI_CONVERTER;
  ```
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:191] The `File` converter uses `newEmptyValueConverter` (no trimming), so a config value like `" /test"` produces `new File(" /test")` with a leading space. The test at `ConvertersTest.java:397` validates this intentionally. This is inconsistent with most other converters (Boolean, numeric types, URI, etc.) which use `newTrimmingConverter`. Consider whether trimming should be applied here for consistency, since accidental leading/trailing spaces in config values are common.
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:193] The `URI` converter uses `newTrimmingConverter(newEmptyValueConverter(URI::create))`, which correctly trims whitespace before parsing. Good choice — URIs should not contain leading/trailing whitespace.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds builtin converters for `File` and `URI` to avoid reflection-based `NoSuchMethodException` overhead in the implicit converter path. The implementation is clean and consistent with existing patterns, but the `Ser.readResolve()` deserialization switch is missing cases for the new converter IDs (and the pre-existing PATH_CONVERTER), which will cause serialization round-trips to fail.

POINTS:
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:877] **Ser.readResolve() missing cases for IDs 18, 19, 20** — The switch statement only handles IDs 0–17 and falls through to `default: throw ConfigMessages.msg.unknownConverterId(id)`. FILE_CONVERTER (id 19) and URI_CONVERTER (id 20) are not added, and PATH_CONVERTER (id 18) was already missing in the base code. Without `case 18: return PATH_CONVERTER;`, `case 19: return FILE_CONVERTER;`, and `case 20: return URI_CONVERTER;`, any serialized wrapper converter delegating to these built-in converters will fail on deserialization. Fix all three in this PR since the pattern was already established by PATH_CONVERTER.
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:191] **FILE_CONVERTER and PATH_CONVERTER intentionally skip trimming** — Both use `newEmptyValueConverter` without `newTrimmingConverter`, preserving leading/trailing whitespace (e.g. `" test"` → `new File(" test")`). This is consistent with `Path` semantics where whitespace can be meaningful in file paths. By contrast, `URI_CONVERTER` wraps with `newTrimmingConverter` (like most other converters), so `" http://localhost"` → `URI.create("http://localhost")`. This design is sound and the tests validate it.
- [implementation/src/test/java/io/smallrye/config/ConvertersTest.java:391-411] **Tests are well-structured** — Both tests verify (1) normal conversion, (2) leading-space handling, (3) that `getConverterOrNull` returns a non-null converter, and (4) that the converter is a `BuiltInConverter` (not the implicit reflection-based fallback). Good coverage for the new functionality.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds builtin converters for `File` and `URI` to avoid NoSuchMethodExceptions for these common use cases. The implementation follows existing patterns well, but the `Ser.readResolve()` serialization switch statement is missing case handlers for the new converter IDs (and also for the existing PATH_CONVERTER).

POINTS:
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:985] **Critical: Missing serialization support for new converters.** The `Ser.readResolve()` switch statement handles IDs 0-17 but does not include cases for ID 18 (`PATH_CONVERTER`), ID 19 (`FILE_CONVERTER`), or ID 20 (`URI_CONVERTER`). When a serialized `BuiltInConverter` with ID 19 or 20 is deserialized, the `default` branch throws `InvalidObjectException: Unknown converter ID`. Add `case 18: return PATH_CONVERTER;`, `case 19: return FILE_CONVERTER;`, and `case 20: return URI_CONVERTER;` to the switch. Note that PATH_CONVERTER (ID 18) has the same pre-existing gap, so adding all three restores consistency and fixes the pre-existing bug too.
- [implementation/src/main/java/io/smallrye/config/Converters.java:191] **Nit: Consider trimming for File converter for consistency.** The `FILE_CONVERTER` uses `newEmptyValueConverter(File::new)` without trimming, while `URI_CONVERTER` wraps with `newTrimmingConverter`. The existing `PATH_CONVERTER` also doesn't trim, so this is consistent with that pattern. However, `File` paths with leading/trailing whitespace could be unexpected. Consider whether trimming should be applied to `File` converter as well for consistency with most other built-in converters (Boolean, Double, Float, Long, Integer, etc. all use trimming).
